question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file where a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction appears at exactly the BatchProperties.maxItems boundary, causing the fee update to be split from its preceding token-creation transaction and processed in a separate database transaction, violating atomicity of fee schedule initialization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE containing a fee collector account ID that requires a slow database lookup, causing the processing to approach the processingTimeout limit and triggering a health check failure that causes the parser to restart and re-process the fee update?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (2s) by submitting a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE followed immediately by a token transfer that depends on the new fee schedule, where the flush interval causes the fee update to not yet be committed when the transfer is processed, resulting in the transfer being assessed with the old fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between isEnabled() returning true and setEnabled(false) being called during parser shutdown, causing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE to begin processing but not complete, leaving the fee schedule in a partially updated state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file where the number of TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions exactly equals BatchProperties.maxItems, causing the entire batch to consist only of fee updates and bypassing any ordering constraints that require fee updates to be processed after their dependent token transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms polling frequency to submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction that arrives in the gap between two polling cycles, causing the parser to process it in the next cycle alongside transactions from a different consensus time window, resulting in incorrect fee effective timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (5m) catch-up batching behavior to cause multiple TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions for the same token to be batched together, where the batch processing does not guarantee ordering, resulting in an older fee schedule overwriting a newer one?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file containing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE with a fractional fee having a denominator of zero, causing an arithmetic exception during processing that consumes the full processingTimeout before failing, effectively blocking the parser from processing legitimate fee updates for 10 seconds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxItems (60,000) limit by submitting a stream file with 59,999 dummy transactions followed by a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE, causing the fee update to be the last item in a batch and potentially subject to a partial commit if the batch transaction rolls back after the fee update is written?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of access control on setEnabled(boolean) in the ParserProperties interface to disable the balance parser (via BalanceParserProperties), causing fee-related balance checks to use stale data and allowing transactions with insufficient balances to appear valid in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (10) by submitting 11 stream files containing TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions, causing the 11th file to be rejected or block, and then submitting a fee-reverting transaction that gets processed before the queued fee updates, resulting in fees being applied in the wrong order?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency polling mechanism to submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction that is processed in the same polling cycle as a TOKENCREATION transaction for the same token, where the fee update is processed before the token creation due to ordering within the batch, causing the fee schedule to be applied to a token that does not yet exist in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (minimum 100ms) by submitting TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions at exactly 100ms intervals, keeping the batch perpetually at the flush boundary and causing fee updates to be committed with maximum latency, during which the mirror node serves incorrect fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE with a deeply nested or circular fee structure that causes the parser to enter an infinite loop during processing, eventually hitting the processingTimeout and causing the parser to mark the stream file as failed, skipping the fee update entirely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.maxFiles (1) and BatchProperties.maxItems (60,000) to cause a scenario where a single large stream file containing both a TOKENCREATION and a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE is processed as two separate batches due to item count limits, with the fee update batch failing and leaving the token without a fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isEnabled() check timing by submitting a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE during a parser restart window where isEnabled() returns false, causing the fee update to be skipped and the mirror node to permanently reflect an outdated fee schedule for the affected token?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (5m) by submitting a stream file that is 5 minutes behind the current consensus time, causing the parser to enter catch-up batching mode and process TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions out of their intended sequence relative to other token operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-based polling (20ms) to create a timing attack where a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE and a subsequent token transfer are submitted in the same 20ms window, causing the parser to process the transfer before the fee update due to internal ordering, resulting in the transfer being assessed with the old fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxItems (60,000) limit by submitting a stream file with exactly 60,000 transactions where the last transaction is a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE, causing the fee update to be processed in isolation in the next batch without the context of the preceding token operations, leading to incorrect fee schedule application?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot Actuator endpoint (if misconfigured) to call setEnabled(false) on the RecordParserProperties bean, disabling TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE processing and causing the mirror node to serve stale fee schedules indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (2s) and queueCapacity (10) interaction to create a denial-of-service condition where the queue fills with non-fee transactions, causing TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions to wait in the queue for up to 20 seconds (10 files × 2s flush interval) before being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE containing a fee collector account ID that does not exist in the mirror node database, causing the parser to spend the full processingTimeout attempting to resolve the entity and then failing, resulting in the fee update being retried indefinitely due to RetryProperties.maxAttempts = Integer.MAX_VALUE?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (5m) to submit a backdated stream file containing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE with an older timestamp, causing the parser to process it in catch-up mode and apply the fee schedule with an incorrect effective timestamp that predates the actual fee change?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency (10ms) to submit TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions faster than the parser can process them, causing a backlog that results in fee updates being applied out of order when the parser catches up, with newer fee schedules being overwritten by older ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxFiles (1) constraint to force the parser to process one stream file at a time, and then submit a stream file containing only a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE without the preceding TOKENCREATION, causing the fee schedule to be applied to a token that the mirror node has not yet indexed?
